## Non-Destructive Control Statement

No UI was changed. No navigation was changed. No permissions were changed. No TXQuote, reporting, documents, scoped services, MGA UI, or end-user behavior was changed. No frontend reads were replaced. No second dry-run was run. No Phase 4B was begun. This report is an audit and containment determination only. Rollback of unauthorized RE-07 stamps is a corrective data operation under the same batch, not a new migration action.

---

## Section 1 — Reconciliation Purpose

RE-07 executed downstream stamping of 21 records across CensusVersion, QuoteScenario, CaseTask, Proposal, EnrollmentWindow, RenewalCycle, ActivityLog, and ExceptionItem entities. These records were stamped with `master_general_agent_id` and `master_group_id` values drawn from the approved NBG and SCP MGA/MasterGroup hierarchy.

This audit determines whether each of the 21 stamped records was:

- **Authorized** — a legitimate downstream record of a BA-09 through BA-12 repaired BenefitCase (live IDs: `cd9a`, `cd9b`, `cd9c`, `cd9d`)
- **Unauthorized (BA-16 overlap)** — downstream record whose `case_id` references a BenefitCase ID in the `cd77`–`cd7a` range (quarantine/block scope per BA-16)
- **Unauthorized (BA-17 overlap)** — downstream record whose `case_id` references a BenefitCase ID that does not exist as a live record (BA-17 quarantine/block scope)
- **Ambiguous** — requiring rollback or quarantine pending further review

---

### BA-17 Quarantine/Block Scope

BA-17 covers downstream workflow records whose `case_id` references BenefitCase IDs that do not exist as live records. By definition, any downstream record carrying `case_id` = cd77, cd78, cd79, or cd7a falls under BA-17.

## Section 4 — P0 Violation Classification

### Finding

**ALL 21 RE-07 stamped records are UNAUTHORIZED.**

Every one of the 21 records stamped during RE-07 carries a `case_id` that references a BenefitCase ID in the `cd77`–`cd7a` placeholder range. None of those BenefitCase IDs exist as live records in the BenefitCase entity. They are the exact IDs covered by the **BA-16 quarantine/block approval** (cd77–cd7a BenefitCase range) and the **BA-17 quarantine/block approval** (21 downstream workflow records referencing missing BenefitCase IDs).

**Zero** of the 21 stamped records are authorized downstream records of the BA-09 through BA-12 repaired BenefitCases (`cd9a`, `cd9b`, `cd9c`, `cd9d`). The live repaired BenefitCases have their own downstream operational records (e.g., the Redwood Dental case at `cd9a` with `employer_group_id: 69e16a7b98a89c653c72cd73`) — those records were NOT stamped during RE-07 because they were not yet identified.

### Root cause of the violation

RE-07 was executed using the dry-run era placeholder `case_id` values (`cd77`–`cd7a`) to identify and stamp downstream records. These placeholder IDs were the case IDs that existed in the seeded sample/dry-run data but do NOT correspond to live BenefitCase records. The correct RE-07 scope should have been restricted exclusively to downstream records whose `case_id` matches the four live repaired BenefitCase IDs (`cd9a`, `cd9b`, `cd9c`, `cd9d`).

### Violation severity

| Classification | Value |
|---|---|
| Violation type | P0 — Remediation execution scope boundary crossed |
| Records unauthorized | **21 of 21** |
| Records authorized | **0 of 21** |
| BA scope violated | BA-16 (cd77–cd7a BenefitCase range) + BA-17 (21 downstream records referencing missing IDs) |
| Corrective action required | **Rollback all 21 RE-07 stamps to prior values (null MGA, null MasterGroup)** |
| RE-08 status | **BLOCKED — must not execute until rollback completes and is confirmed** |

---

## Section 6 — Corrected RE-07 Scope Definition

After rollback, RE-07 must be re-scoped and re-executed against only records whose `case_id` matches one of the four live repaired BenefitCase IDs:

| case_id | Case | MGA | MasterGroup |
|---|---|---|---|
| `69e16a3998a89c653c72cd9a` | NBG-1001 (Redwood Family Dental) | NBG | NBG-MG-001 |
| `69e16a3998a89c653c72cd9b` | NBG-1002 (Pacific Harbor Tech) | NBG | NBG-MG-001 |
| `69e16a3998a89c653c72cd9c` | SCP-2001 (Summit Outdoor Supply) | SCP | SCP-MG-001 |
| `69e16a3998a89c653c72cd9d` | SCP-2002 (Front Range Manufacturing) | SCP | SCP-MG-001 |

A corrected RE-07 execution must:
1. Query each downstream entity type filtered by the above four `case_id` values
2. Identify which records exist with those live case IDs
3. Stamp only those records
4. Confirm no overlap with cd77–cd7a range before stamping

The live BenefitCase records at `cd9a`–`cd9d` do have some downstream records (e.g., `69efe29ffecddbea94de8002` Redwood has two CensusVersions, `69e16cc064b94008398a8846` Pacific Harbor has an ActivityLog) — but the corrected RE-07 must query by the `cd9a`–`cd9d` case IDs specifically, not the old placeholder IDs.

## Section 9 — Required Owner Decision Before Rollback Execution

The 21-record rollback is a corrective action under the existing batch. It does not create new scope, does not quarantine records, and does not advance the migration. It strictly reverts the unauthorized stamps.

**Rollback does not require a new BA approval item** — it is a mechanical correction of an execution error within the approved batch boundary.

**However:** Before the corrected RE-07 is re-executed (against the live cd9a–cd9d case IDs), a brief owner confirmation is recommended to confirm:
1. The rollback of all 21 unauthorized stamps is approved
2. The corrected RE-07 scope (live case IDs cd9a–cd9d only) is understood and approved
3. Continuation to RE-08 after corrected RE-07 is approved
